2605.22775.title: MambaGaze: Bidirectional Mamba with Explicit Missing Data Modeling for Cognitive Load Assessment from Eye-Gaze Tracking Data
2605.22775.paper_summary: Real-time cognitive load assessment from eye-tracking signals could potentially enable adaptive human-centered-AI such as safety-critical applications such as driver vigilance monitoring or automated flight deck assistance, yet two challenges persist: handling frequent data missingness from blinks and tracking failures, and efficiently modeling long-range temporal dependencies. We propose MambaGaze, a framework that addresses these challenges through 1) XMD encoding, which augments raw features with observation masks and time-deltas to explicitly model data uncertainty, and 2) bidirectional Mamba-2, which captures temporal dependencies with linear computational complexity. Experiments on CLARE and CL-Drive datasets under leave-one-subject-out evaluation show that MambaGaze achieves 76.8% and 73.1% accuracy, respectively, outperforming CNN, Transformer, ResNet, and VGG baselines by 4-12 percentage points. Edge deployment benchmarks on NVIDIA Jetson platforms demonstrate real-time inference at 43-68 FPS with power consumption below 7.5W, confirming feasibility for wearable cognitive load monitoring.
2605.22775.paper_summary_zh: 即時的認知負荷評估來自眼動追踪信號，可能使以人為中心的自適應人工智慧成為可能，例如安全關鍵應用，如駕駛員警覺性監測或自動化飛行甲板輔助，但仍然存在兩個挑戰：處理因眨眼和追踪失敗而頻繁出現的數據缺失，以及有效建模長期時間依賴性。我們提出了MambaGaze，這是一個通過1) XMD編碼來解決這些挑戰的框架，該編碼通過觀察掩碼和時間差來增強原始特徵，以明確建模數據不確定性，以及2) 雙向Mamba-2，該模型以線性計算複雜度捕捉時間依賴性。在CLARE和CL-Drive數據集上進行的留一主體評估實驗顯示，MambaGaze分別達到76.8%和73.1%的準確率，超過CNN、Transformer、ResNet和VGG基準4-12個百分點。在NVIDIA Jetson平台上的邊緣部署基準測試顯示，實時推斷達到43-68 FPS，功耗低於7.5W，確認了可穿戴認知負荷監測的可行性。
2605.22775.author: Amir Mousavi et.al.
2605.22775.authors: Amir Mousavi, Mohammad Sadegh Sirjani, Erfan Nourbakhsh, Mimi Xie, Rocky Slavin, Leslie Neely, John Davis, John Quarles
